title: Epipactis kleinii
intro: Epipactis kleinii, also known as Klein's helleborine, is a rare, terrestrial orchid species native to Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis kleinii include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Epipactis kleinii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Epipactis kleinii is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Epipactis, is commonly known as the helleborines and is native to Europe, North Africa, and Asia. Epipactis kleinii is further classified as a species within the genus Epipactis. The plant belongs to the order Asparagales and is often found in woodland environments and along rivers. As a species of orchid, Epipactis kleinii is considered to be a type of terrestrial orchid.
